TSLA.title: Elon Musk Says Tesla Sales 'Actually Increased' When Trump Removed $7,500 EV Credit, Calls Government Incentives Claim 'Totally False'
Elon Musk Gets Shout Out From Strategy's Michael Saylor For 'Historic' SpaceX IPO: '25% Of The Mag8 Now Holds Bitcoin'
Ro Khanna Questions Wall Street Folks Celebrating Elon Musk For Creating 4,000 Millionaires: Did They 'Celebrate' Biden?

macro_events.event_summary: An Iran-U.S. peace deal triggered a risk-on rally in Asia, with SoftBank up 12%+ and broad equity strength.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium compression (lower oil, lower VIX)
Risk-on rotation into high-beta growth and Iran-exposed names
Potential lifting of Iran sanctions reopening oil supply and trade

beneficiaries.rationale: Southwest is the most jet-fuel-sensitive US airline (largest fuel cost as % of opex, limited hedging in recent years); lower crude from Iran supply normalization drops fuel costs directly to EBIT.
Delta has heavy international exposure including Middle East codeshares; lower fuel + reopened Iranian airspace shortens Europe-Asia routes, reducing fuel burn and crew costs.
Booking Holdings derives ~50% revenue from EMEA; geopolitical de-escalation lifts travel demand into Middle East and removes a key tail risk on European summer bookings.
Carnival has the largest fuel exposure among cruise operators and heavy Mediterranean itineraries; lower bunker fuel + safer East Med routing is a direct margin tailwind.

losers.rationale: Iran sanctions relief would add 1-1.5 mbpd of crude back to global supply, pressuring Brent and Exxon's upstream realizations directly; largest US integrated with highest absolute oil leverage.
Highest oil price beta among US large-cap E&Ps (Permian pure-play, high debt load makes equity more leveraged to crude); Iran supply return is a direct headwind to cash flow.
Lockheed is the prime contractor on THAAD, PAC-3, and F-35 sales to Gulf states; a durable Iran-US peace reduces urgency of Middle East missile defense and FMS pipeline.
Raytheon's Patriot and missile defense backlog has been heavily driven by Iran threat; de-escalation pressures the defense rotation that has been a multi-year tailwind.
Northrop is a peer-rotation loser as defense sector de-rates on reduced Middle East threat premium; less direct exposure than LMT/RTX but moves with the basket.

headlines: SoftBank surges more than 12% as Iran-U.S. peace deal sends Asia stocks soaring
